title: श्री अयप्पा अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अयप्पा अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
विष्णुशम्भुसुतं देवं शास्तारं प्रणमाम्यहम्।
भूतनाथं महादेवं शबरीगिरिवासिनम्॥

॥ नामावली ॥
ॐ महाशास्त्रे नमः।
ॐ महादेवाय नमः।
ॐ महादेवसुताय नमः।
ॐ अव्ययाय नमः।
ॐ लोककर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ लोकभर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ लोकहर्त्रे नमः।
ॐ परात्पराय नमः।
ॐ त्रिलोकरक्षकाय नमः।
ॐ धन्विने नमः।
ॐ तपस्विने नमः।
ॐ भूतनाथाय नमः।
ॐ समस्तदेवतासौख्यदायकाय नमः।
ॐ विष्णुपुत्राय नमः।
ॐ शिवपुत्राय नमः।
ॐ मोहिनीसुताय नमः।
ॐ हरिहरपुत्राय नमः।
ॐ अयप्पाय नमः।
ॐ अरिविमर्दनाय नमः।
ॐ धर्मशास्त्रे नमः।
ॐ वीरशास्त्रे नमः।
ॐ यतिवर्याय नमः।
ॐ नैष्ठिकब्रह्मचारिणे नमः।
ॐ शबरीगिरिनिवासाय नमः।
ॐ पम्पानदीतीरवासाय नमः।
ॐ भक्तानुकम्पिने नमः।
ॐ भगवते नमः।
ॐ भक्तवत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ पापविनाशनाय नमः।
ॐ आपद्बान्धवाय नमः।
ॐ कुमाराय नमः।
ॐ मणिकण्ठाय नमः।
ॐ मणिवर्णाभरणधारिणे नमः।
ॐ मायानटकसूत्रधाराय नमः।
ॐ केरलविख्याताय नमः।
ॐ सर्वरोगविनाशनाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वमङ्गलदायकाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वेश्वराय नमः।
ॐ सर्वभूतहिते रताय नमः।
ॐ सर्वज्ञाय नमः।
ॐ सर्वपापहारिणे नमः।
ॐ नागराजसुपूजिताय नमः।
ॐ नागाभरणभूषिताय नमः।
ॐ वनवासिने नमः।
ॐ वरदाय नमः।
ॐ वेदवेदाङ्गपारगाय नमः।
ॐ गजारुढाय नमः।
ॐ गणेशसहोदराय नमः।
ॐ कार्तिकेयसहजाय नमः।
ॐ पन्तलराजपुत्राय नमः।
ॐ राजशेखराय नमः।
ॐ राज्यरक्षकाय नमः।
ॐ सुब्रह्मण्यसखाय नमः।
ॐ महामणिमालाधारिणे नमः।
ॐ वन्दितलोकत्रयाय नमः।
ॐ घण्टाकर्णसंहारिणे नमः।
ॐ महिषासुरमर्दनाय नमः।
ॐ वीरबाहवे नमः।
ॐ वीरमार्तण्डाय नमः।
ॐ वीरसिंहासनाय नमः।
ॐ अग्निज्वालावलयाय नमः।
ॐ त्रिनेत्राय नमः।
ॐ बालरूपाय नमः।
ॐ काननवासिने नमः।
ॐ कल्याणदायकाय नमः।
ॐ श्रमविनोदनाय नमः।
ॐ शत्रुसंहारकारिणे नमः।
ॐ धर्मसंरक्षकाय नमः।
ॐ धैर्यशालिने नमः।
ॐ कृपानिधये नमः।
ॐ ज्योतिर्मयाय नमः।
ॐ त्रिदशपूजिताय नमः।
ॐ शरणागतवत्सलाय नमः।
ॐ भगवते भूतनाथाय नमः।
ॐ चन्द्रमौळिने नमः।
ॐ कलादूराय नमः।
ॐ मन्त्रतन्त्रस्वरूपाय नमः।
ॐ मकरज्योतिर्दर्शनाय नमः।
ॐ मकरसंक्रमणोत्सवाय नमः।
ॐ पतिताधराय नमः।
ॐ पावनाय नमः।
ॐ पद्मासनस्थितपरात्पराय नमः।
ॐ अनादिनिधनाय नमः।
ॐ सुमुखाय नमः।
ॐ सुकुमाराय नमः।
ॐ सुप्रसन्नाय नमः।
ॐ महाबलाय नमः।
ॐ अजेयाय नमः।
ॐ यतीन्द्राय नमः।
ॐ प्रसन्नवदनाय नमः।
ॐ जगदीशाय नमः।
ॐ जनार्दनाय नमः।
ॐ ज्ञानसम्पन्नाय नमः।
ॐ अखण्डाय नमः।
ॐ आत्मसंस्थिताय नमः।
ॐ जगदाधाराय नमः।
ॐ यजुर्वेदनिवासाय नमः।
ॐ सामगानप्रियाय नमः।
ॐ नीलकण्ठप्रियपुत्राय नमः।
ॐ गिरिजासुताय नमः।
ॐ अनन्तकोटिब्रह्माण्डनायकाय नमः।
ॐ नित्यब्रह्मचारिणे नमः।
ॐ ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवात्मकाय नमः।
ॐ श्रीधर्मशास्त्रे नमः।
ॐ शबरीमलैनाथाय नमः।
ॐ लोकवीराय नमः।
ॐ आपत्बन्धवे नमः।
ॐ श्री शास्त्रे नमः।

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
अयप्पस्याष्टशतकं नामानि यः पठेत् सदा।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तः शास्तुः सायुज्यमाप्नुयात्॥

मण्डलकालपठनाद् विशेषफलमाप्नुयात्।
शबरीमलयात्रायां सर्वविघ्ननिवारणम्॥

॥ इति श्री अयप्पा अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Vishnushambhusutam Devam Shastaram Pranamaamyaham.
Bhutanatham Mahadevam Shabarigirinivasinam.

|| Namavali ||
Om Mahashastre Namah.
Om Mahadevaya Namah.
Om Mahadevasutaya Namah.
Om Avyayaya Namah.
Om Lokakartre Namah.
Om Lokabhartre Namah.
Om Lokahartre Namah.
Om Paratparaya Namah.
Om Trilokarakshakaya Namah.
Om Dhanvine Namah.
Om Tapasvine Namah.
Om Bhutanathaya Namah.
Om Samastadevasaukhyadayakaya Namah.
Om Vishnuputraya Namah.
Om Shivaputraya Namah.
Om Mohinisutaya Namah.
Om Hariharaputraya Namah.
Om Ayyappaya Namah.
Om Arivimardanaya Namah.
Om Dharmashastre Namah.
Om Virashastre Namah.
Om Yativaryaya Namah.
Om Naishtikabrahmacharine Namah.
Om Shabarigirinvasaya Namah.
Om Pampanaditiravasa Namah.
Om Bhaktanukampine Namah.
Om Bhagavate Namah.
Om Bhaktavatsalaya Namah.
Om Papavinashanaya Namah.
Om Apadbandhavaya Namah.
Om Kumaraya Namah.
Om Manikanthaya Namah.
Om Manivarnabharanadhaarine Namah.
Om Mayaanatakasutraadharaya Namah.
Om Keralavikhyataya Namah.
Om Sarvarogavinashanaya Namah.
Om Sarvamangaladayakaya Namah.
Om Sarveshvaraya Namah.
Om Sarvabhutahite Rataya Namah.
Om Sarvajnaya Namah.
Om Sarvapapahaarine Namah.
Om Nagarajasupujitaya Namah.
Om Nagabharanabhushitaya Namah.
Om Vanavasine Namah.
Om Varadaya Namah.
Om Vedavedangaparagaya Namah.
Om Gajarudhaya Namah.
Om Ganeshasahodaraya Namah.
Om Kartikeyasahajaya Namah.
Om Pantalarajaputraya Namah.
Om Rajashekharaya Namah.
Om Rajyarakshakaya Namah.
Om Subrahmanyasakhaya Namah.
Om Maha-manimala-dharinae Namah.
Om Vanditalokatrayaya Namah.
Om Ghantakarnasahaarine Namah.
Om Mahishasuramardanaya Namah.
Om Virabahave Namah.
Om Viramartandaya Namah.
Om Virasimhasanaya Namah.
Om Agnijvalavalayaya Namah.
Om Trinetraya Namah.
Om Balarupaya Namah.
Om Kananavasine Namah.
Om Kalyanadayakaya Namah.
Om Shramavinodanaya Namah.
Om Shatrusamharakarine Namah.
Om Dharmasamrakshakaya Namah.
Om Dhairyashaline Namah.
Om Kripanidhaye Namah.
Om Jyotirmayaya Namah.
Om Tridashapujitaya Namah.
Om Sharanagata-vatsalaya Namah.
Om Bhagavate Bhutanathaya Namah.
Om Chandramauline Namah.
Om Kaladuraya Namah.
Om Mantratantraswarupaya Namah.
Om Makarajyotirdarshanaya Namah.
Om Makarasankramanotsavaya Namah.
Om Patitadharaya Namah.
Om Pavanaya Namah.
Om Padmasanasthita-paratparaya Namah.
Om Anadinidhanyaya Namah.
Om Sumukhaya Namah.
Om Sukumaraya Namah.
Om Suprasannaya Namah.
Om Mahabalaya Namah.
Om Ajeyaya Namah.
Om Yatindraya Namah.
Om Prasannavadanaya Namah.
Om Jagadishaya Namah.
Om Janardanaya Namah.
Om Jnanasampannaya Namah.
Om Akhandaya Namah.
Om Atmasamsthitaya Namah.
Om Jagadadharaya Namah.
Om Yajurvedanivasaya Namah.
Om Samaganapriyaya Namah.
Om Nilakanthapriyaputraya Namah.
Om Girijasutaya Namah.
Om Anantakotibrahmandanayakaya Namah.
Om Nityabrahmacharine Namah.
Om Brahmvishnushivatmakaya Namah.
Om Shri Dharmashastre Namah.
Om Shabarimalainathaya Namah.
Om Lokaviraya Namah.
Om Apatbandhave Namah.
Om Shri Shastre Namah.

|| Phalashrutih ||
Ayyappasya-ashtashatakam Namani Yah Pathet Sada.
Sarvapapavinirmuktah Shastuh Sayujyam-aapnuyat.

Mandalakala-pathanad Visheshaphalam-aapnuyat.
Shabarimalayatrayam Sarvavighna-nivaranam.

|| Iti Shri Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: विष्णु और शम्भु (शिव) के पुत्र, शास्ता, भूतनाथ, महादेव, शबरीगिरि (शबरिमला) निवासी भगवान अयप्पा को प्रणाम।

१०८ नामों में प्रमुख: महाशास्ता (महान शासक), महादेवसुत, विष्णुपुत्र, शिवपुत्र, मोहिनीसुत, हरिहरपुत्र (हरि-हर दोनों के पुत्र), धर्मशास्ता, वीरशास्ता, नैष्ठिकब्रह्मचारी, शबरीगिरिनिवासी, मणिकण्ठ (मणिमाला धारी), भक्तवत्सल, आपद्बन्धु, पन्तलराजपुत्र, महिषासुरमर्दन, मकरज्योतिर्दर्शन (मकरसंक्रान्ति की ज्योति), नित्यब्रह्मचारी, शबरीमलैनाथ, लोकवीर।

फलश्रुति: जो इन अष्टोत्तर शत नामों का सदा पाठ करता है वह सर्वपापमुक्त होकर शास्ता (अयप्पा) का सायुज्य प्राप्त करता है। मण्डलकाल (41 दिन व्रत) में पाठ से विशेष फल और शबरीमला यात्रा में सर्वविघ्ननिवारण होता है।
benefits: Contains 108 sacred names of Lord Ayyappa (Dharma Shasta)
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants union with lord ayyappa
Special results when recited during Mandala Kalam (41-day vrat)
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles during sabarimala pilgrimage
Powerful prayer for Naishtika Brahmacharya and spiritual discipline
description: Dedicated to Lord Ayyappa, the Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali (श्री अयप्पा अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) is a devotional recitation of 108 sacred names revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Bhuta Natha Upakhyana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all sins and grant union with Lord Ayyappa and remove all obstacles during Sabarimala pilgrimage. It is also said to special results when recited during Mandala Kalam (41-day vrat). This prayer is traditionally recited on Saturday (Shanivar) and Monday (Somvar) and during Makar Sankranti and Mandala Puja to invoke divine grace. At 108 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 12 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali - 108 names of Lord Ayyappa in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred namavali for Sabarimala devotees.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali (श्री अयप्पा अष्टोत्तर शतनामावली) is a sacred recitation of 108 divine names of Lord Ayyappa — also known as Dharma Shasta, Hariharaputra (son of Hari/Vishnu and Hara/Shiva), Manikantha, and Sabarinath — the presiding deity of Sabarimala temple in Kerala. Each name is chanted with the prefix Om and suffix Namah, traditionally recited during archana (offering of flowers, kumkum, or tulsi at each name) at Ayyappa temples and home shrines. The 108 names extol Ayyappa's birth from Vishnu and Shiva, his role as the celibate (naishtika brahmachari) protector of dharma, his abode at Shabari Giri, his vehicle Vagheshwara (tiger), and his grace upon devotees who undertake the 41-day Mandala vrat. (Sources: Bhuta Natha Upakhyana; Sabarimala temple archana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the most important names in the Ayyappa 108 Namavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Among the 108 names, the most cherished and frequently invoked are: Mahashasta (the great teacher and ruler), Hariharaputra (son of Vishnu and Shiva), Mohini-suta (born of Vishnu's Mohini form), Manikantha (the one with a bell around his neck — referencing his foundling story at Pandalam), Dharma Shasta (the lord of dharma), Naishtika Brahmachari (the eternal celibate), Shabari-giri-nivasa (resident of Shabari hill), Bhutanatha (lord of all beings), Pampa-nadi-tira-vasa (dweller on the banks of Pampa river), Pantala-rajaputra (foster son of the king of Pandalam), Makara-jyotir-darshana (revealer of the Makara Jyoti light), and Vagheshwara (lord with the tiger). These names trace Ayyappa's entire divine biography and theology. (Sources: Bhuta Natha Upakhyana; Sabarimala tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali used in archana?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical archana procedure is: after morning bath and wearing clean clothes (preferably black or saffron during Mandala vrat), the devotee sits before an Ayyappa idol or picture, lights a ghee or coconut-oil deepam, and offers either tulsi leaves, lotus or red flowers, or red kumkum at the feet of the deity while reciting each of the 108 names. Each name should be chanted clearly with the Om prefix and Namah suffix. The recitation is concluded with the phalashruti verse and the famous chant Swamiye Saranam Ayyappa. The archana is especially prescribed during the 41-day Mandala Kalam (mid-November to late December), on Makar Sankranti, every Saturday, and during pilgrimage preparation for Sabarimala. (Sources: Sabarimala paddhati; Ayyappa Bhakti Mandali tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of reciting Ayyappa 108 names during Mandala Kalam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mandala Kalam is the 41-day vrat (vow) period observed by Ayyappa devotees, beginning from Vrishchika Sankramanam (mid-November) and culminating with Mandala Puja and Makara Jyoti darshan at Sabarimala in late December and mid-January. During these 41 days, the devotee observes naishtika brahmacharya (strict celibacy), wears black or blue, eats one simple meal a day, sleeps on the floor, performs puja morning and evening, and recites Ayyappa namavali, ashtakam, and chalisa daily. Reciting the 108 names during this period is believed to remove all sins (sarvapapa-vinashana), grant union with Lord Ayyappa (Shastuh sayujyam), and remove all obstacles during the difficult Sabarimala pilgrimage through forest paths to the hilltop shrine. (Sources: Sabarimala Mandala vrat paddhati; Bhuta Natha Upakhyana phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Ayyappa and what is the Hariharaputra story?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Ayyappa is the divine son of Lord Vishnu (in his Mohini form) and Lord Shiva — hence the name Hariharaputra (son of Hari and Hara). The puranic account narrates that when Lord Vishnu took the bewitching female form of Mohini to defeat the asura Bhasmasura, Lord Shiva became enchanted by her beauty, and from their divine union was born Ayyappa to destroy the demoness Mahishi (sister of Mahishasura). The infant Ayyappa was found by King Rajashekhara of Pandalam in Kerala on the banks of the Pampa river, with a small bell around his neck — earning the name Manikantha (jeweled neck). After fulfilling his divine mission, Ayyappa established his abode at Shabari Giri (Sabarimala) as the eternal celibate Dharma Shasta. (Sources: Bhuta Natha Upakhyana; Brahmanda Purana; Pandalam royal tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: On which days and festivals is Ayyappa Ashtottara most auspicious to recite?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Ashtottara Shatanamavali is most auspiciously recited on: every Saturday (Shanivar — Ayyappa's principal day), every Monday (Somvar — sacred to Shiva his father), throughout the 41-day Mandala Kalam vrat (mid-November to late December), on Mandala Puja day (last day of the 41-day vrat), Makar Sankranti (January 14-15) coinciding with Makaravilakku and Makara Jyoti at Sabarimala, Vishu (Kerala new year), and during the Pratistha Dinam of the Sabarimala temple. Devotees undertaking the Sabarimala pilgrimage recite the 108 names morning and evening throughout the 41-day vrat, and during the actual climb up the 18 sacred steps (Pathinettam Padi) to the sannidhanam. (Sources: Sabarimala temple calendar; Drikpanchang Ayyappa festival dates.)
